language.name: Eldarindëva
language.description: The living tongue shared across all branches of the Eldarindë, from the Taralindë of the High Realm to the Sylvarindë of the deep forests. Warm, vowel-rich, and melodic, with a flowing cadence suited to song, poetry, and formal address. Distinguished from ancient forms by softer consonant clusters and a preference for open syllables. Regional dialects exist among the wood elves and drow, but the written standard remains unified.
language.note: Names of the Elyndarûm (the divine pantheon) are sacred names that exist outside the normal linguistic rules and should not be derived from or decomposed into standard roots.

consonants.description: Always hard, as in 'cat'. Never soft.
As in 'dawn'. Only appears word-initially or after n.
Always hard, as in 'gift'. Never soft.
Soft aspiration, as in 'home'. Silent between identical vowels.
Always clear and light, as in 'lily'. Never dark.
Light single tap, as in Spanish 'pero'. Never harsh or trilled.
Light and dental, as in French 'tu'.
As in 'yes'. Consonantal only before vowels.
Palatalized l, as in Italian 'figlio'. Rare, poetic.

vowels.description: Open, as in 'father'. The most common vowel.
Open-mid, as in 'bed'. Clear and warm.
Always pronounced as a distinct syllable, never silent. Used word-finally.
Open-mid, as in 'for'. Round and full.
Long u, held longer. Rare, used in sacred or ancient words.

diphthongs.description: Falling, as in 'aisle'. Very common, the signature Arandori sound.
Two syllables flowing together. As in 'idea' but smoother.

phonotacticRules.description: Every syllable contains exactly one vowel or diphthong. Initial consonant is optional. Final consonant is optional but limited to: n, r, l, s, th.
Stress falls on the second-to-last syllable. Words ending in -ë shift stress to the third-to-last syllable. Monosyllabic words are stressed.
Only these consonant clusters may begin a syllable. All other syllables begin with a single consonant or a vowel.
Syllables (and words) may only end in these consonants, or in a vowel. Most words end in vowels.
These sounds or sequences do not exist in Arandori. The sounds x, j (as in 'judge'), w, and z never appear.
Within a single root, front vowels (e, i) and back vowels (o, u) tend not to mix. The vowel 'a' is neutral and combines freely with any vowel. Compound words may cross harmony boundaries at the join.
When a voiceless consonant (t, s, th) meets a voiced consonant (d, v) at a compound boundary, the voiceless softens: t+v becomes 'lv', s+d becomes 'nd'.
Most Arandori words end in a vowel. Masculine-coded words often end in -on, -or, -ar, -en. Feminine-coded words often end in -ë, -a, -iel, -iel. Neuter words end in -in, -il, -al.

wordOrder.description: Everyday speech uses Verb-Subject-Object. Formal address, law, and military commands use Subject-Object-Verb. Poetry allows free order since noun cases prevent ambiguity.

articles.description: 'I' (the) precedes the noun. 'Na' (a/an) precedes the noun. Omitted in poetry and proper nouns.

nounCases.description: Subject of the sentence. Unmarked base form.

nounCases.example: aranor = of the king / the king's

plurals.description: Standard plural adds -i (aran > arani, kings). Collective -ath denotes 'all of a kind' (elenath, all the stars). Dual -at for exactly two (aranat, two kings).
rules: Words ending in -a change to -ai (fanya > fanyai)
Words ending in -ë change to -i (dorë > dori)
Words ending in a consonant add -i (elen > eleni)

adjectives.agreement: Adjectives agree with noun number but not case.
adjectives.formation: Many adjectives formed with suffix -ea (morëa = shadowy, from mor = shadow) or -in (valin = eternal, from val = enduring).

verbs.negation: Prefix ú- before the verb: útirna = does not watch.

compoundFormation.description: The modifying element comes first. 'Star-shield' = ael (star) + thalon (shield) = aelthalon. At compound boundaries, consonant clusters may simplify per phonotactic rules.
joiningRules: If the first root ends in a consonant and the second begins with one, insert linking vowel -a- or -i-: thal + nor = thalanor
If both elements end/begin with vowels, the first vowel may elide: aeli + elen = aelelen or aelelen
The suffix -ion/-arion denotes 'son of / bearer of': calarion = light-bearer
The suffix -iel/-ithiel denotes 'daughter of / maiden of': eleniel = star-maiden
The suffix -ndor/-dor denotes 'land of / realm of': arandor = high-realm
The suffix -rindë denotes 'kindred / people / folk': eldarindë = elf-kindred, sylvarindë = forest-kindred
The suffix -va/-ëva denotes 'speech of / pertaining to / of the nature of': Eldarindëva = speech of the elven-kind
The suffix -ath denotes collective totality: elenath = all the stars, eldarionath = all noble elves

pronouns.note: Often dropped; verb suffix implies subject

entries.etymology: Primordial root. One of the oldest words in the language.
Primordial root associated with visible light and divine illumination.
Primordial root. Connotes active defense rather than passive shelter.
Primordial root. Neutral connotation; shadow is not inherently evil in Arandori thought, merely the complement of light.
Primordial root associated with moonlight and reflected starlight.
Primordial root. Carries connotations of both temporal permanence and spiritual sanctity.
Primordial root. The Arandori do not distinguish between the sound of flowing water and music.
Extended form of ael with individualizing suffix -en.
From ancient root dor- (settled place) + nominalizing -ë.
From ara- (noble, high) + -n (agent). One who is noble.
Primordial root. Implies watchful protection rather than aggressive defense.
From van- (fair, blessed) + -ye (abstract quality).
Onomatopoeic origin, evoking the soft sound of cloth or mist moving.
From er- (lone, singular) + -yn (quality). Originally 'singular in ability.'
Primordial root. Used as both standalone adjective and productive prefix.
Primordial root. Distinct from dorë (realm); nor is the physical earth itself.
Primordial root associated with both physical and spiritual might.
Primordial root. Carries connotation of immensity and depth.
From nau- (heat, warmth) + -r (intensifier).
Used as collective suffix: elenath = all the stars.
From rim- (outer) + -a (nominalizer).
Ancient compound os- (wall) + to- (stand). A thing that stands walled.
From mi- (in, within) + na- (upward). That which rises from within.
From thal (protection) + -on (concrete object suffix).
From cal (light) + -a (noun form). The tangible presence of light.
From mor (shadow) + -ë (noun form).
From sil (silver) + -më (emanation, quality that radiates).
From ael (star) + lin (water). Star-water: where stars are reflected.
From lin (song) + dalë (craft, making).
Root dal- (to shape, to make) + -ë.
aran (king/high) + dorë (realm). The name of the elvish civilization itself.
elda (elf, star-person) + -inë (collective people).
ael (star) + lin (song/linking) + dor (protector). A shield that sings with starlight.
cal (light) + -arion (bearer/carrier).
vael (wind) + lis (keen) + -ar (instrument).
eryn (swift) + dor (one who acts).
tir (to watch) + -ion (great/mighty thing).
sil (silver) + var (eternal) + -on (entity).
aer (sky/upper air) + an (toward) + dor (lord/ruler).

definitions: name of a stealth frigate class
entries.etymology: thal (shield) + anis (silence, stillness).

definitions: name of a light frigate class
entries.etymology: thalas (sea-shield, from thal + uin) + rën (light/lesser).
tal (to carry, to bear) + asir (faithfully, steadily).
var (sacred/eternal) + dal (craft) + -um (substance). Literally: eternally-crafted substance.
From elen (star) + -da (being). A being of starlight. The base word for all elvenkind.
From rin- (to join, to gather) + -dë (collective noun). Used as a suffix to form the names of elvish peoples and houses.
elda (elf) + rindë (kindred). All those who share elven blood. Also the root of the language name: Eldarindëva (speech of the elven-kind).
From tar- (high, lofty, crowned) + -a. Distinct from ara (noble); tara implies formal sovereignty and the right to rule.
tara (crowned/sovereign) + lindë (from lin, song + rindë, kindred, contracted). The elves who bear the crown. The formal name of the High Elven race.
cal (light) + var (eternal) + rindë (kindred). The eternally radiant kindred. A poetic alternative name for the High Elves.
elda (elf) + arion (noble bearer) + -ath (collective). All the noble-born elves. A formal, archaic name for the High Elves.
From sil- (gleam) shifting to syl- (green-gleam) + -va (domain). The shimmering green depths of ancient forest.
sylva (forest) + rindë (kindred). The kindred of the deep forest. The formal name of the Wood Elven race.
From um- (beneath) + -bra (darkness, intensified mor). A deeper, more absolute darkness than morë.
umbra (deep shadow) + rindë (kindred). The kindred who dwell in deep shadow. The formal name of the Drow.
aurë (day/dawn) + -va (pertaining to). Used in the compound Aurë-elves: elves of the dawn, keepers of ancient knowledge.
From ael (star) with consonant shift l>r denoting the medium rather than the point of light.
From va- (movement) + ael (sky). The sky in motion.
From tau- (tall, growing) + -r (place).
Extended form of uin with poetic vowel expansion.
Onomatopoeic: the soft opening of a petal.
From gal- (to grow) + -adh (living entity).
From ith- (pale) + sil (silver). The silver pale one.
From an- (golden/warm) + -or (great one).
Primordial root for water in its flowing, drinkable form.
Onomatopoeic: the sound of stone struck.
Primordial root. The animating essence of a living being.
Primordial root. The material housing of the fëa.
From oi- (ever) + -alë (state of being).
An elvish measure of time, far longer than a mortal year.
From au- (sun, warmth) + -rë (period).
From lóm- (twilight) + -ë.
From anor (sun) + -rë (moment/event). The sun's event.
From an (toward) + dún (west/setting) + -ë.
From her- (to rule) + -u (agent).
Feminine form of heru.
amba- (highest) + aran (king). Established AEN rank.
elda (star-person) + mar (home/command). Established AEN rank.
rimba (fleet, many ships) + -len (master). Established AEN rank.
rimba (fleet) + -aer (lesser/subordinate). Established AEN rank.

thal (shield) + -ion (great one). One who is a great shield.
From oht- (war) + -ar (agent).
Ancient root cir- (to cut through) + -ya (thing). That which cuts the void.
From rim- (many, numerous) + -ba (collective of vessels).
From mac- (to cut) + -il (instrument).
From eht- (to pierce) + -ar (instrument).
Onomatopoeic: the sound of a bowstring.
Primordial root for love in its deepest sense.
mel (love) + -da (state of being).
From al- (great) + cal (light) + -ar. Great radiance.
From es- (to begin) + tel (end/purpose). Trust that the beginning leads to a worthy end.
From fai- (fade) + -rë (entity).
From sér- (to rest, to be still) + -ë.
From nol- (to know) + -më (abstract quality).
From sai- (to understand deeply) + -la (having the quality of).

mor (shadow) + -ëa (adjectival, 'having the quality of').
cal (light) + -ima (capable of, full of).
From tul- (to stand firm) + -ca (quality).
From hos- (to collect) + -ta (verbal).

From ran- (to flow, to move freely) + -ya (verbal).
From cen- (to perceive) + -ta (verbal). To make perceived.

From lin (song) + -da (verbal action).
From or (upon/above) + -ta (verbal).
From lan- (down) + -ta (verbal).
From tul- (to come) + -ya (causative). To cause to come.
From has- (to cleave through) + -ta (verbal). Originally 'to cut through water,' now applied to void-travel.
From lui- (deep) + -na (colored). Established in month name Luinavas.
From sil (silver) + quen (bright). Established in month name Silquendil.
From lis- (sweet) + -ë (quality). Established in month name Lissuinion.
From ur- (fire/heat) + ith (sky) + -ë. Established in month name Urithiel.
From naur (fire) + -vion (diminishing). Established in month name Narviondë.
From myn- (memory) + dar (to linger). Established in month name Myndarial.

From mor (shadow) + il (light) + thë (state). Established in month name Morilthië.
From al- (to grow tall) + -da (living thing).
From lai- (green/growing) + -rë (season).
From hrív- (chill) + -ë.
From yav- (fruit, harvest) + -ië (season).
From tui- (to sprout) + -li (beginning).
Primordial root.
Primordial root. Applied to any object of great value.
From cui- (to awaken) + -vë (event/moment).
Primordial root.
From oth- (outer) + -ar (person).
From nil- (friend) + -më (abstract quality).
From nil- (friend) + -dë (person).
From ves- (to bind) + -ta (act). A binding act.
From her- (to rule) + -ë (abstract).
From cán- (to order/command) + -o (agent).

definitions: one newly sworn to the fleet
entries.etymology: From gwer- (to begin, to set out) + -eth (novice). AEN Navy rank E-1.
From tûr- (mastery, skill) + wen (young person). AEN Navy rank E-2.
From mae- (skilled) + -gor (hand/doer). A skilled hand. AEN Navy rank E-3.
From car- (to do, to make) + -n (agent). One who does. AEN Navy rank E-4.
From rath- (to course, to set in order). One who keeps order. AEN Navy rank E-5.
From ber- (valor) + -eth (one who holds). One who holds valor. AEN Navy rank E-6.
From naur (fire) + -on (great one). A keeper of the flame of duty. AEN Navy rank E-7.
From dûr- (deep, enduring) + -n (agent). One of deep experience. AEN Navy rank E-8.
From ael (star) + -gor (hand/doer). A hand guided by stars. AEN Navy rank E-9.
From ael (star) + aran (noble) contracted. Born under the stars of command. AEN Navy rank O-1.
From elen (star) + -or (of). Of the stars. AEN Navy rank O-2.
From fae- (spirit, fairness) + -l (agent). One of fair spirit. AEN Navy rank O-3.
From mith- (grey, experienced) + rand (wanderer/voyager). AEN Navy rank O-9.
From aran (king) + -dur (servant/steward). The king's steward of the void. AEN Navy rank O-12.
From cau- (to begin anew) + -r (agent). One who begins. AEN Marine rank E-1.
caur (recruit) + -en (proven). A recruit who has been tested. AEN Marine rank E-2.
From hol- (to close, to seal) + -len (one who). One who closes ranks. AEN Marine rank E-3.
From bran- (to endure, to hold fast) + -non (person). AEN Marine rank E-4.
From thal (shield) + -nd (standing). One who stands as a shield. AEN Marine rank E-5.
From ron- (to arch, to span) + -d (solid). A solid arch. AEN Marine rank E-6.
From mab- (hand, grasp) + ur- (fire). Hand of fire. AEN Marine rank E-7.
From vor- (ever-faithful, steadfast) + -on (one). The ever-faithful one. AEN Marine rank E-8.
From gar- (to hold, to possess) + aran (lord). One who holds lordship among soldiers. AEN Marine rank E-9.
From var (eternal/sacred) + -eth (one who holds). One who holds the eternal watch. AEN Marine rank E-10.
From cal (light) + -ar (agent). Bearer of specialized light. AEN Marine rank O-1W.
From nim- (white, pale, new). A fresh spark of command. AEN Marine rank O-1.
From ril- (to glitter, to gleam). AEN Marine rank O-2.
From gal- (to shine, radiance) + -ad (standing). Shining in place. AEN Marine rank O-3.
From dor (land/realm) + -en (of). One of the realm. AEN Marine rank O-4.
From cer- (to encircle, to fortify) + -in (quality). One who fortifies. AEN Marine rank O-5.
From eor- (lone, solitary peak) + -th (standing). A lone mountain of authority. AEN Marine rank O-6.
From thel- (purpose, intent) + -ion (great one). One of great purpose. AEN Marine rank O-7.
From or- (above, upon) + -on (great). A great height. AEN Marine rank O-8.
From am- (upward) + -on (place). A high place of command. AEN Marine rank O-9.
From ara (noble/high) + -ath (collective). The height of all nobility. AEN Marine rank O-10.
From val (mighty) + nor (land). Mighty lord of all the land. AEN Marine rank O-11.
Primordial copula. The simplest assertion of existence.
From an- (toward) + -ta (verbal). To move toward another.
From map- (to grasp) + -a (verbal).
From les- (to move onward) + -ta (verbal).
From tul- (to come) + -a (verbal). Base of tulya (to lead, causative).
From cen- (to perceive) + -a (verbal). Broader than tir (to watch/guard).
From las- (ear, to listen) + -ta (verbal).
From har- (to sit, to settle) + -a (verbal).

From yes- (first, initial) + -ta (verbal).
From nur- (deep, hidden) + -ta (verbal).
From rauc- (to crack) + -a (verbal).
From pan- (to spread wide) + -ta (verbal).
From hol- (to close, to seal) + -ma (verbal).

From col- (to bear) + -a (verbal). Related to talasir (burden-bearer).
From lor- (to dream, to slumber) + -na (verbal).
From cui- (to awaken) + -va (verbal). Related to cuivë (awakening).
From yal- (to cry, to summon) + -la (verbal).
From es- (being, identity) + -ta (verbal). To give essence.
From en- (re-, again) + vanya (to go). To go again.
From naur (fire) + -a (verbal). To be as fire.
From vil- (air, flight) + -ya (verbal).
From tan- (to forge, to fashion) + -a (verbal). Related to tanar (war-captain, one who forges).
From fir- (to fade away) + -ia (verbal). The gentle Arandori term for death.
From sin- (to go steadily) + -na (verbal).
From nor (land) + -ta (verbal). To course across the land.
From cal (light) + -ta (verbal). To produce light.

Primordial root for the female of the species.

From mas- (to knead, to prepare) + -sa (result).
From mir (precious) + uvor (liquid). Precious liquid.
From ser- (to flow) + -cë (substance).

Primordial root. The physical and spiritual center.
From cas- (top, crest).

From run- (to fly swift and straight) + -ya (thing).

From par- (to arrange, to compose) + -ma (thing).

From cov- (to shelter) + -a (place).
From ham- (to clothe) + -ma (material). The clothing of war.

From al- (to grow tall) + -ta (quality). Having grown to greatness.

From san- (to affirm, to know surely) + -da (quality).
From fir- (to fade) + -in (state). Having faded from life.
From li- (many, multiple) + -ë.
From sin- (small) + -omë (measure).
From dín- (silence) + -a (quality).
From er- (one, lone) + -essë (in state of). In the state of being one.
From ain- (before all) + -ëa (quality). Older than yára; predating memory itself.
From laurë (gold, golden light) + -a (adjectival).
From cú (bow) + -na (quality). Having the quality of a bow.
From mal- (gold as material) + -da (made of).
From nor- (hard, knotted) + -na (quality).
From lar- (to rush) + -ca (quality). Swifter than eryn; implies urgency.
From yan- (to join, to connect) + -da (structure). That which joins two sides.
Primordial root. A word spoken that binds the soul.
From sérë (peace) + vesta (oath). A peace sealed by oath.
From centa (speech) + -ar (agent). One who speaks on behalf of a realm.

From hérë (authority) + mar (house). The house of authority.
From hérë (authority) + -ath (collective). Any formal gathering that holds collective authority. The generic term; contrast with Valarindë for the specific supreme council.

definitions: the Council of the Seven Great Houses
supreme governing council of the Arandorë Eldainë
entries.etymology: From val (mighty/divine) + rindë (kindred/folk). The mighty kindred; the seven ruling houses gathered as one voice. Proper noun: always capitalized as 'the Valarindë' on subsequent reference.
From tara (high/royal) + halla (tall/lofty). The exalted seat.
From cáno (commander) + -va (pertaining to). That which pertains to command.
From san- (to arrange, to order) + -yar (established thing). An established ordering.
From san- (to arrange) + -no (agent). One who arranges what is just.
From han- (to understand, to feel for) + -yalë (abstract quality). The quality of compassionate understanding.
From ranya (to wander) + -ar (agent). One who wanders for commerce.
From mir (precious/jewel) + -lë (abstract). A precious offering.
From var (sacred/eternal) + -ro (agent). One who serves the sacred.
From var (sacred) + -riel (feminine agent). A woman who serves the sacred.

definitions: restorer of peace to the body
entries.etymology: From sérë (peace/rest) + -no (agent). One who brings peace to the afflicted.
From nolmë (knowledge) + -tar (high agent). A high keeper of lore.

definitions: one who watches what shall be

From man- (to bind) + -dos (enclosed place). A place of binding.

From tara (high/royal) + corma (ring). The royal ring worn upon the brow.
From mir (precious) + -ien (small thing). A small precious thing.
From panta (to open) + cor (round). The round thing that opens.
From asc- (hard inner) + -a (thing).
From hel- (to cover) + -vë (surface).
From thúl- (to breathe) + -ë (thing). Related to fëa (soul) through breath.
From nír- (to weep) + -ë (thing).
From olor- (to dream) + -ë (thing). A vision of sleep.
From naic- (to bite, to sting) + -ë (thing).
From sang- (to corrupt) + -wa (liquid).
From sérë (peace) + lossë (blossom). A blossom that brings peace to the body.
From ros- (to scatter from above) + -së (substance).
From raum- (to rage) + -o (entity). A raging entity.
From vael (wind) + raumo (storm). The storm's wind-voice.
From cal (light) + raumo (storm). The light of the storm.
From los- (white, pure) + -së (substance). Pure white substance.
From hel- (cold, frozen) + -cë (substance).
From lith- (grey remnant) + -i (thing).
From usqu- (to rise darkly) + -ë (thing).
From tol- (to stand alone) + -lë (place). A place that stands alone.
From imb- (between, in the gap) + -a (place).
From falas- (shore, coast) + -së (formation).
From hrót- (to hollow) + -a (place). A hollowed place.
From an- (to be truly) + -wa (state). The state of being real.
From fui- (to twist, to distort) + -lë (thing).
From nó- (deep, inner) + -lë (thing). A deeply hidden thing.
Primordial root. The weight of what must be.
From ler- (to release, to free) + -ya (state).
From moi- (to labor under) + -a (state).
From ner- (to do what is required) + -më (abstract).
From anta (to give) + -vë (act of). The act of giving fully.
From envanya (to return) + -lë (abstract). The quality of returning.
From aic- (sharp, keen) + -alë (abstract pursuit). The sharp pursuit of justice.
From han- (to understand) + -yassë (state of grace). The grace of understanding.
From har- (to sit, to remain) + -më (quality). The quality of remaining.
From ohtar (warrior) + cárë (making). The making of endless conflict.
From san- (to arrange) + -yalë (abstract quality). The quality of right arrangement.
From rai- (to reach beyond) + -në (thing). A thing beyond reach.
From al- (great) + mar (self/home) + -éa (state). The state of seeing oneself as great.
From nú- (beneath) + -tëa (state). The state of placing oneself beneath.
From is- (to know) + -ta (verbal). Deeper than cena (to see); knowing with the mind.
From tec- (to mark) + -a (verbal).
From cen- (to perceive) + -ta (verbal causative). To cause another to perceive.
From fas- (to weave) + -ta (verbal).
From or- (above, upward) + -ta (verbal). To move upward.
From cai- (to lay down) + -ta (verbal).
From ler- (to release) + -ya (verbal).
From tul- (to lead) + -ta (causative). To lead another to knowledge.
From par- (to arrange, compose) + -ya (verbal).
From ais- (holy breath) + -ta (verbal).
From ruh- (to darken upon) + -ta (verbal).
From ava- (away from) + tyar- (cause). To cause blame to go away.
From en- (re-, again) + vanya (to go). To go again.
From nam- (to weigh, to measure) + -ma (verbal).

definitions: to walk in the steps of
entries.etymology: From hil- (to follow) + -ya (verbal).
Primordial root. To move from here to there.
From nol- (knowledge) + -ya (verbal). To acquire knowing.
From lin (song) + -da (verbal). To produce song.
From hos- (to heap, to bring together) + -ta (verbal).
From rin- (to recall) + -ta (verbal).
From hes- (to single out) + -ta (verbal).
From tara (high) + -ya (verbal). To make oneself the highest.
From lanta (to fall) + -lda (willingly). To fall of one's own will.
From taracorma (crown) + -ya (verbal). To bestow the crown.
From nírë (tear) + -ya (verbal). To produce tears.
From nos- (kin, clan-root) + -të (formal collective). A formal kinship group with political standing. Distinct from mar (dwelling).

definitions: one of the seven ruling houses
entries.etymology: From ara (high/noble) + nostë (house). A house of the highest rank.
From hên (child) + -ion (great one/bearer). The child who bears the mantle.
From nos- (kin) + sírë (flowing line). The flowing line of kinship.
From nostë (house) + sercë (blood) + -ion (great). The great blood-house.
From nor (land) + mar (dwelling). The land-dwelling; the totality of a house's domain.

definitions: one who shelters beneath another's shield
entries.etymology: From nur- (beneath) + thal (shield). One who stands beneath the shield of a lord.
From thal (shield) + tir (to watch/guard). The shield-watcher; the one who guards from above.
From tara (high/royal) + -no (agent). One who attends the royal.
From mar (house) + tir (to guard). The guardian of the house.
From hérë (authority) + cáno (commander). The commander of the realm's governance.
From nor (land) + tir (to guard). The guardian of the land.
From anta (to give) + -më (abstract). The act of giving across generations.
From nossírë (lineage) + cár (making/course). The course of lineage.
From yesta (to begin) + nermë (duty). The duty that begins at birth.

definitions: heraldic device of a noble house
entries.etymology: From nostë (house) + calma (light-mark). The light-mark of a house; the emblem that identifies.
From ranel (banner) + -lië (art/collection). The art and system of noble identification.

definitions: the pull of a body upon another
entries.etymology: From lung- (heavy, to bear down) + -ë (thing). The quality of bearing down. The Arandori understanding of gravitational attraction.
From lungë (weight/pull) + sanyar (law). The law of weight. The formal term for gravitational theory in Arandori natural philosophy.

definitions: that which causes motion or change
entries.etymology: From tulca (strong/firm) + -lë (abstract quality). The abstract quality of strength applied; any cause of change in motion.
From cuivë (awakening/life) + -lë (quality). The life-quality; that which awakens or moves things. Encompasses kinetic, thermal, and radiant energy.

definitions: the measure of matter in a body
entries.etymology: From ondo (stone/material) + -lë (measure). The stone-measure; how much substance a thing contains. Distinguished from lungë (weight) which depends on context.
From ondo (stone) + hosta (to gather). How tightly the substance is gathered.

definitions: the curved track a body follows around another
entries.etymology: From tir (to watch/circle) + -iel (daughter/thing born of). The path born of watching; the path a lesser body traces around a greater. Named for the observation that orbits were first understood by watching.
From quer- (to turn) + -ië (continuous action). The continuous turning.

definitions: the rhythmic rise governed by celestial bodies
entries.etymology: From uin (great water) + -lië (rhythmic pattern). The rhythmic pattern of the great waters.
From sír- (to flow in a direction) + -ë (thing). A directed flow; applies to water, air, and energy.
From fal- (to foam, to crest) + -ma (thing). A cresting thing; applies to ocean waves, sound, and light.
From falma (wave) + querië (rotation/turning). How often the wave turns back upon itself.

definitions: the full range of light separated into its parts
entries.etymology: From cala (light) + cárië (spreading/making). The spreading of light; when white light is separated into its constituent colors.
From mor (darkness) + vael (air/sky) + -ë (place). The dark sky; the space between celestial bodies. Not simply darkness but the medium (or lack thereof) through which light and ships travel.

definitions: cloud of luminous gas from which stars are born
entries.etymology: From fanya (cloud/mist) + calë (light). A luminous cloud. The stellar nurseries.

definitions: a point of infinite density from which no light returns
entries.etymology: From lungë (gravity/weight) + tumna (deep/profound). The deepest gravity; the point beyond which substance collapses past understanding.

definitions: one of the irreducible kinds of matter
entries.etymology: From er- (single, alone) + -thë (essence). A single essence; a substance that cannot be broken into simpler parts.

definitions: the smallest particle of an element
entries.etymology: From erthë (element) + -in (diminutive). The smallest grain of an element.

definitions: anything that has mass and occupies space
entries.etymology: From ondo (stone/material) + -assë (general category). The general category of material; all that is substantial.
From sanmë (thought) + tírië (watching/observation). A thought built from watching; a model of understanding tested against what is observed.

definitions: an invariant principle observed in nature
entries.etymology: From erthë (element/fundamental) + sanyar (law). A fundamental law; a principle that holds without exception across all observation.

definitions: a deliberate watching under controlled conditions
entries.etymology: From centa (to speak/declare) + tírië (watching). A watching that speaks; an observation designed to answer a question.
From tir (to watch) + -ië (continuous act). The continuous act of watching.

definitions: the act of assigning number to observation
entries.etymology: From not- (to count, to reckon) + -iër (process). The process of counting.
From not- (to count) + -ië (art/practice). The practice of counting; mathematics.
From hen (eye) + calassë (glass). Eye-glass; a shaped transparent medium.

definitions: instrument for observing distant celestial bodies
entries.etymology: From ael (star) + tírion (place of watching). The star-watching instrument.
From tië (path) + hir (to find) + -ë (thing). The path-finding thing.
From ista (to know) + calma (device/mark). A device for knowing; any scientific instrument.

definitions: directed flow of energy through space
entries.etymology: From cala (light) + sírë (current). A current of light; any electromagnetic radiation.

definitions: a disturbance in the fabric of space caused by massive bodies
entries.etymology: From lungë (gravity) + falma (wave). A wave of gravity; predicted by Arandori field theory and observed by sensitive instruments.

definitions: the woven structure of space and time through which all things move
entries.etymology: From morvailë (void/deep space) + fassë (cloth/fabric). The fabric of the void; the unified structure of space and time.

definitions: a vast congregation of stars bound by gravity
entries.etymology: From ael (star) + rindë (kindred/folk). A kindred of stars; the great families of light. The Arandori see a galaxy as a nation of stars.

definitions: of or pertaining to the galaxy
entries.etymology: From aelrindë (galaxy) + -a (adjectival). Of the galaxy.

definitions: a star and all bodies bound to it
entries.etymology: From ael (star) + ondë (dwelling-domain). The domain of a star.

definitions: a body that circles a star
entries.etymology: Primordial root. Any world; a body massive enough to shape itself round and clear its path.
From cemen (planet) + yára (old/ancient). The ancient world; one's world of origin.
From ael (star) + falma (wave/crest). A cresting star; a body that streams light behind it.

definitions: a minor body of rock drifting in the void
entries.etymology: From ondo (stone) + ranya (to wander). A wandering stone.

definitions: a burning streak across the sky
entries.etymology: From naur (fire) + runya (arrow). A fire-arrow; a fragment that burns on entering atmosphere.

definitions: a named figure drawn among the stars
entries.etymology: From ael (star) + tir (to watch) + -ion (great figure). A great figure seen in the stars; a pattern named for watching.

definitions: the act of sailing through the void
entries.etymology: From hasta (to sail) + -ië (continuous act). The ongoing act of sailing the void.
From vinya (new/fresh) + hasta (to sail). The new sailing; the method of folding spacetime rather than crossing it. Distinguished from conventional hasta.

definitions: the folded void through which ships travel at superluminal speed
entries.etymology: From vinyahasta (warp) + -lë (state/domain). The domain of warp; the folded void.

definitions: a controlled source of immense energy
entries.etymology: From naur (fire) + ondo (stone/core). The fire-stone; the heart of a ship or installation that produces power.

definitions: a device that converts energy into motion
entries.etymology: From tulca (strong/force) + -më (device/place). The force-making device.

definitions: the central power source of a vessel
entries.etymology: From cuivëlë (energy) + hón (heart). The energy-heart.

definitions: the device that produces defensive energy barriers
entries.etymology: From thal (shield) + dalë (craft/device). The shield-craft.

definitions: an instrument that perceives beyond the eye
entries.etymology: From tir (to watch) + -ilmë (instrument). The watching instrument.

definitions: a device for transmitting voice across distance
entries.etymology: From centa (to speak) + -ilmë (instrument). The speaking instrument.
From centa (speech) + sírë (current). A current of speech; directed information flow.

definitions: the outer structure of a vessel
entries.etymology: From cirya (ship) + helvë (skin). The skin of the ship.

definitions: an internal structural barrier within a vessel
entries.etymology: From cirya (ship) + thal (shield) + -lë (structure). The shield-wall within a ship.

definitions: an enclosed bay for smaller vessels within a larger one
entries.etymology: From cirya (ship) + cova (harbor). A harbor within a ship.

definitions: a sealed passage between atmosphere and vacuum
entries.etymology: From morvailë (void) + ando (gate). The gate to the void.

definitions: a long path within a structure
entries.etymology: From tië (path) + hallë (long/tall). A long path.

definitions: a new land claimed and inhabited
entries.etymology: From cemen (planet) + norë (land/region). A new land on a world.

definitions: a small settlement established for vigilance
entries.etymology: From tir (to watch) + norë (land). A land of watching.

definitions: a constructed habitation in orbit or deep space
entries.etymology: From aer (sky) + halla (tall/lofty). A lofty dwelling; a structure that hangs in the sky.

definitions: a structure or platform in orbit around a body
entries.etymology: From tíriel (orbit) + mar (dwelling). A dwelling in orbit.

definitions: a sentient being from beyond one's own people
entries.etymology: From othar (stranger) + nossë (kin/people). Strange kin; one who is kin in sentience but stranger in origin.
From nossë (kin/clan) + -a (category). A category of kinship; a biological kind.

definitions: a people who have built governance and culture
entries.etymology: From hérë (governance) + ondë (domain). A governed domain; a people with law, craft, and culture.

definitions: a bounded region of galactic space
entries.etymology: From ael (star) + dorë (realm/territory). A realm of stars; an administrative or navigational division of space.
From naur (fire) + ross- (to feed) + -a (substance). That which feeds the fire.
From cuivëlë (energy) + tulcë (strength). Energy-strength; usable power.
From tulcalë (force) + thal (shield). A shield of force; any projected energy field.

definitions: the assembled people who serve aboard a vessel
entries.etymology: From cirya (ship) + hosta (to gather). The ship's gathered people.

definitions: numerical positions that identify a point in space
entries.etymology: From tírië (observation) + not (counting/number). Numbers from observation; positional values.

definitions: the art and science of finding one's way through the void
entries.etymology: From tië (path) + ista (to know). The knowing of paths.

definitions: the calculated course of a moving body
entries.etymology: From tië (path) + sírë (current). The current of the path; the directed line of travel.

definitions: the envelope of air surrounding a world
entries.etymology: From vael (wind/air) + aer (sky). The air-sky; the breathable shell of a world.

definitions: the component of air that sustains life
entries.etymology: From thúlë (breath) + vael (air). The breath-air; the vital component.

definitions: the absence of matter and atmosphere
entries.etymology: From vailë (void) + morë (absence/dark). The empty void; true nothingness.

definitions: the remains of a destroyed ship
entries.etymology: From rauca (to break) + hasta (sail/voyage). A broken voyage; ship debris.
From hir (to find) + hasta (voyage). Found from a voyage; recovered material.
Primordial root for the male parent.
Primordial root for the female parent.
From yon- (to spring from) + -do (masculine).
From yel- (to spring from) + -dë (feminine).
From tor- (to stand alongside) + -on (masculine).
From net- (to bond with) + -të (feminine).
From ver- (to bond in oath) + -no (masculine agent).
From ves- (to bond in oath) + -së (feminine).
From vesta (oath) + -ar (agent). One bound by oath.
From vesta (oath) + -ië (ceremony/act). The oath ceremony.

definitions: one who came before in the bloodline
entries.etymology: From nossë (kin) + atar (father). A kin-father; one of the line before.

definitions: one who follows in the bloodline
entries.etymology: From aps- (to follow after) + -en (one).

definitions: one who has lived long and carries wisdom
entries.etymology: From yára (old/ancient) + -no (agent). An ancient one.
From vinya (new) + hên (child). A new child.

definitions: the weight of having fallen short
entries.etymology: From nairë (sorrow, lament) + -lë (quality). The quality of sorrowful self-reproach.

definitions: sorrow for what was done or left undone
entries.etymology: From en- (back/again) + yalië (calling). Calling back; wishing to undo.
From mil- (to desire wrongly) + -vë (state).
From hanta (to thank) + -lë (quality). The quality of giving thanks.

definitions: the state of having lost all hope
entries.etymology: From oien- (to go out, extinguish) + calë (light). When the light goes out.

definitions: bittersweet longing for what once was
entries.etymology: From yára (old) + mellë (love/affection). Love for the old; aching fondness for the past.

definitions: a communication set down in marks
entries.etymology: From teca (to write) + -ië (thing made). A written thing.
From centa (to speak) + -ssë (transmission). A transmitted speech.

definitions: unverified information that flows like a stream
entries.etymology: From lasta (to hear) + sírë (current). A current of hearing; what flows from ear to ear.

definitions: a formal account of what has been witnessed
entries.etymology: From tírië (observation) + centa (speech). An observation spoken formally.
From canva (decree) + centa (speech). A decreed speech.
From maquet- (to ask) + -ië (act). The act of asking.
From han- (to understand) + quet- (to speak) + -ië (act). To speak understanding; to reply.
From mor (shadow/darkness) + -na (quality).
From lossë (snow) + -a (adjectival).
From cálë- (to grow green) + -a (adjectival).

definitions: the color of soil and bark
entries.etymology: From var- (earthy) + -në (quality).

definitions: the color of dusk and royalty
entries.etymology: From tui- (dusk-glow) + -në (quality).
From sind- (pale, washed) + -a (quality).
Primordial root for a measured portion of time.

definitions: the smallest perceptible span of time
entries.etymology: From lúmë (hour) + -itë (diminutive). A tiny hour.
From yén (year/age) + rindë (folk/cycle). A cycle of ages.
From yén (year) + ambë (great measure). A great measure of years.
From anarë (dawn/sunrise) + -ya (coming). The coming dawn.
From yára (old) + anarë (dawn). The old dawn.
Primordial temporal particle.
From en- (toward) + yárë (time-span). Toward the near time.
From oialë (eternity). As eternity; without ceasing.
From ú- (negation) + oialë (forever). Not-ever.

definitions: the encirclement and starving of a position
entries.etymology: From hosta (to gather) + -ië (sustained act). A sustained gathering around.
From nurta (to hide) + mahta (to fight). Hidden fighting.
From en- (back) + hasta (to sail). To sail back; to withdraw.
From arya (forward) + hasta (to sail). To sail forward.
From rima (border/edge) + -lda (face/side). The side-edge.
From rauca (to break) + naurë (fire). Breaking fire.

definitions: combat to seize an enemy vessel
entries.etymology: From cirya (ship) + mahta (to fight). Ship-fighting; to fight aboard.

definitions: the closing of routes by force
entries.etymology: From cirya (ship) + thal (shield/barrier). A shield of ships.
From tírië (watching) + hasta (to sail). A watching voyage.
From tir (to guard) + cirya (ship). Guard-ships.
From hir (to find) + tírië (observation). Finding by watching.
From vesta (oath) + rauca (to break). Oath-breaking; the gravest crime.
From mandos (prison) + -hon (one within). One within the prison.

definitions: a device that performs calculation and reasoning
entries.etymology: From sanmë (thought) + dalë (craft/device). A thought-craft; a machine that thinks.

definitions: a crafted mind; a made sentience
entries.etymology: From fëa (soul/spirit) + dalë (craft). A crafted soul. The Arandori term reflects their philosophical discomfort: is a made mind truly a soul?

definitions: an image made of shaped light
entries.etymology: From cala (light) + hroa (body). A body of light.

definitions: an unmanned device that watches or acts remotely
entries.etymology: From tir (to watch) + nossë (servant-thing). A watching servant.

definitions: the joining of flesh and machine
entries.etymology: From ondassë (matter) + fëa (soul). Matter given soul; the fusion of organic and artificial.

definitions: the art of hiding meaning within signs
entries.etymology: From nólë (secret) + calma (mark). Secret marks; concealed meaning.
From ista (to know) + -ssë (substance). The substance of knowing; raw information.

definitions: an interconnected system of data flow
entries.etymology: From ista (knowledge) + sírë (current). A current of knowledge; connected pathways of information.
From sérë (peace/rest) + dalë (craft). The craft of restoring peace to the body.
From hroa (body) + tírië (observation). Observation of the body.
From ang- (iron, hard) + -ë (substance).

definitions: a mixture of metals forged as one
entries.etymology: From angë (steel) + minya (combined/first). Blended steel; metals joined.

definitions: superheated matter in an energized state
entries.etymology: From naur (fire) + oss- (substance). Fire-substance; matter heated beyond solid, liquid, or gas.

definitions: substance opposed to normal matter; annihilates on contact
entries.etymology: From ú- (negation) + ondassë (matter). Not-matter; the mirror of substance.

definitions: a heavy weapon that projects destructive energy
entries.etymology: From rauca (to break) + naur (fire) + -ion (great instrument). The great fire-breaker.
From tírië (watching) + naurion (fire-thrower). A watching fire-thrower; one that turns to face its target.

definitions: a self-guided projectile launched through the void
entries.etymology: From hasta (to sail) + runya (arrow). An arrow that sails itself.

definitions: a weapon that projects focused energy
entries.etymology: From calasírë (radiation) + -ion (instrument). The radiation instrument; a directed energy weapon.

definitions: a substance that releases energy violently
entries.etymology: From rauca (to break) + erthin (atom/grain). A grain that breaks apart; material that detonates.
From sanyalë (order) + nolmë (knowledge). Ordered knowledge; the science of valid inference.
From mâ (hand, action) + nolmë (knowledge). Knowledge of the hand; how one ought to act.
From aic- (keen/sharp) + calma (light-mark). A keen light; moral sharpness.

definitions: the inner voice that knows right from wrong
entries.etymology: From hón (heart) + sanmë (thought). The thought of the heart.
From estel (hope/faith) + -lië (holding). The holding of faith.
From sanda (true) + calë (light). True light; the clarity of certain knowledge.

definitions: the state of not being certain
entries.etymology: From ú- (negation) + ista (to know) + -ië (state). The state of not-knowing.

definitions: the counted value of a thing
entries.etymology: From mir (precious) + notië (counting). The precious reckoning; what a thing is counted as worth.
From mir (precious) + -ath (collective). The totality of one's precious things.
From ú- (negation) + mirath (wealth). Not-wealth.
From dalë (craft) + -ath (collective). The collective of a craft.

definitions: the origin from which a living thing grows
entries.etymology: From er- (single/first) + -dë (small thing). The first small thing.

definitions: the part that holds to earth
entries.etymology: From tal- (to grip) + -ca (part).

definitions: the light-catching part of a plant
entries.etymology: From las- (green, to catch light) + -së (thing).
From ol- (to grow outward) + -va (extension).

definitions: the ripened yield of a plant
entries.etymology: From yav- (to yield) + -ë (thing). Related to yavië (autumn).
From sor- (to soar on high) + -on (great creature).
From lóc- (to coil) + -ë (creature).

definitions: a great beast of flame and wing
entries.etymology: From ráma (wing) + cal (fire/light) + lócë (serpent). A winged fire-serpent.

definitions: where land or void meets sky
entries.etymology: From aer (sky) + rima (border). The border of the sky.
From ranc- (to reach) + -ë (limb).
From tel- (to stand, to walk upon) + -cë (limb).
From lep- (to touch, to pick) + -së (small part).
From rám- (to extend for flight) + -a (limb).

definitions: a hard growth for cutting food
entries.etymology: From nel- (to bite, to cut) + -et (point).

definitions: a hard projection from the head
entries.etymology: From til- (to point upward) + -dë (growth).
Interrogative particle for time.
From man- (what) + -en (cause/dative). By what?
Partitive particle.
From il- (everything) + -ya (encompassing).
From ná (to be). It is.
Conditional particle. Expresses possibility without commitment.
From er- (one/single). Just one; nothing more.

definitions: the means by which a vessel moves
entries.etymology: From norta (to drive/push) + -lë (system). The driving system.

definitions: a high-power engine that burns fuel into directed thrust
entries.etymology: From naur (fire) + tulcë (thrust/strength). Fire-thrust; the primary sublight drive.

definitions: a membrane that catches starlight for propulsion
entries.etymology: From anor (sun) + cala (light) + fassë (cloth). Sun-light-cloth.

definitions: the merging of light elements to release energy
entries.etymology: From erthin (atom) + yesta (to begin/join). The joining of atoms.

definitions: a reactor that produces energy through atomic fusion
entries.etymology: From erthin (atom) + hón (heart). The atom-heart; the fusion powerplant.

definitions: a device that holds energy for rapid release
entries.etymology: From cuivëlë (energy) + cova (vault/harbor). An energy vault.

definitions: a rotating device that stores kinetic energy
entries.etymology: From querië (rotation/spin) + cova (vault). A spin-vault.

definitions: a sensor that uses reflected waves to detect objects
entries.etymology: From falma (wave) + tir (to watch) + -ilmë (instrument). A wave-watching instrument.

definitions: a sensor that uses reflected light pulses to map surroundings
entries.etymology: From cala (light) + tir (to watch) + -ilmë (instrument). A light-watching instrument.

definitions: a device that captures visual information
entries.etymology: From cena (to see) + -ilmë (instrument). A seeing instrument.

definitions: an instrument that separates light to reveal composition
entries.etymology: From cala (light) + quer- (to turn/split) + -ilmë (instrument). A light-splitting instrument.

definitions: an instrument that detects magnetic or energy fields
entries.etymology: From tulcathal (energy field) + tir (to watch) + -ilmë (instrument). A field-watching instrument.

definitions: a device that senses specific phenomena
entries.etymology: From hir (to find) + -ilmë (instrument). A finding instrument.

definitions: the use of signals and energy to deceive, disrupt, or deny enemy systems
entries.etymology: From centa (signal/speech) + mahta (to fight). Signal-fighting; battle in the unseen spectrum.

definitions: a device that drowns or obscures enemy signals
entries.etymology: From centa (signal) + nurta (to hide). A signal-hider.

definitions: a system or action taken to defeat an enemy weapon or sensor
entries.etymology: From en- (against) + thal (shield) + -ië (act). A counter-shielding act.

definitions: a false target deployed to mislead enemy sensors
entries.etymology: From wanwa (illusion/false) + hroa (body). A false body.

definitions: a focused beam weapon that projects sustained energy at range
entries.etymology: From cala (light) + hasta (to project/sail). A projected light; the primary directed-energy weapon type of the AEN.

definitions: a self-propelled projectile that seeks its target
entries.etymology: From tië (path) + hasta (to sail) + runya (arrow). A path-sailing arrow; a guided projectile.

definitions: short-range weapon systems that protect against incoming threats
entries.etymology: From thal (shield) + tir (to watch) + -ilmë (instrument). The shield-watching instrument; last-line defense.

definitions: any object launched with force at a target
entries.etymology: From tulcalë (force) + runya (arrow). A force-arrow.

definitions: a dense projectile designed to punch through armor
entries.etymology: From ter- (through/piercing) + cáno (sharp one). The through-piercer.

definitions: a discarding carrier that accelerates a penetrator rod
entries.etymology: From rauca (to break away) + runya (arrow). The shell that breaks away; a saboted round.

definitions: projectiles and charges stored for weapons
entries.etymology: From runya (arrow) + cova (store/vault). The arrow-store.

definitions: a mechanism that loads weapons without manual effort
entries.etymology: From naurion (cannon) + pant- (to feed/fill). The cannon-feeder.

definitions: the directing tube of a weapon
entries.etymology: From naurion (cannon) + ossë (tube/channel). The fire-channel.

definitions: sustained fire intended to pin or deny movement
entries.etymology: From nurta (to pin/hide) + naurë (fire). Hiding-fire; fire that forces the enemy to cover.
From tur- (to endure/protect) + -ma (thing). The enduring thing; protective covering.

definitions: armor designed to erode and carry away energy
entries.etymology: From lanta (to fall away) + turma (armor). Falling armor; armor that sacrifices itself.

definitions: a hard heat-resistant material made from baked earth
entries.etymology: From ondo (stone) + cal- (to fire/heat). Fired stone.

definitions: armor composed of bonded layers of different materials
entries.etymology: From parma (layer/page) + turma (armor). Layered armor.

definitions: an interlocking grid pattern, often crystalline
entries.etymology: From raic- (to weave in pattern) + -ossë (structure). A woven structure.

definitions: a material grown as one unbroken crystal lattice
entries.etymology: From er (one/single) + calassë (crystal/glass). A single crystal.

definitions: armor layer applied to a surface
entries.etymology: From turma (armor) + helvë (skin). Armor-skin.

definitions: a porous material that absorbs kinetic energy
entries.etymology: From lungë (weight/impact) + massë (soft substance). Impact-softener.

definitions: a protective layer applied to armor surfaces
entries.etymology: From turma (armor) + fassë (cloth/covering). Armor-covering.

definitions: an interconnected network of shield emitters
entries.etymology: From thal (shield) + raicë (web/lattice). A shield-web.

definitions: a shield projected as a ring or disc around a vessel
entries.etymology: From thal (shield) + rindë (circle/ring). A shield-ring.
From cala (light) + cárië (making) + -a (adjectival). Light-wrought.

definitions: a fast combat craft designed to pursue and destroy threats
entries.etymology: From ráma (wing) + cirya (ship). A wing-ship; a swift attack vessel.

definitions: a vessel designed to carry troops or cargo
entries.etymology: From cola (to carry/bear) + cirya (ship). A bearing-ship.

definitions: a warship assigned to escort and patrol duties
entries.etymology: From tir (to guard) + cirya (ship). A guard-ship.

definitions: a small combat vessel for direct engagement
entries.etymology: From mahta (to fight) + cirya (ship/craft). A fighting craft.

definitions: the quality of warmth or burning
entries.etymology: From ur- (fire/heat) + calë (radiance). Fire-radiance.

definitions: a structure that dissipates waste heat into space
entries.etymology: From uru (heat) + lanta (to fall/shed) + -ilmë (device). A heat-shedding device.

definitions: a substance circulated to absorb and remove heat
entries.etymology: From ringa (cold) + -ssë (substance). Cold-substance.

definitions: the process of drawing heat away
entries.etymology: From ringa (cold) + -lë (process). The cold-making process.

definitions: a forward-facing scoop that captures gas for processing
entries.etymology: From thúlë (breath) + mapë (to seize/grab). A breath-seizer.

definitions: the creation of a genetic duplicate
entries.etymology: From onta (to create/beget) + -lë (process). The begetting process; making a copy of life.

definitions: restoring a thing to working condition
entries.etymology: From envanya (to renew/restore) + -lë (act). The act of renewal.

definitions: the act of connecting vessels or berthing at a station
entries.etymology: From cirya (ship) + vest- (to join/bond). Ship-joining.
From turma (armor) + roch (steed/vehicle). An armored steed; a ground war-machine.

definitions: any craft that carries people or cargo over ground
entries.etymology: From norta (to drive) + roch (steed). A driven steed.
From querië (rotation) + naurion (cannon). A spinning cannon-mount.

definitions: a sealed doorway in hull or armor
entries.etymology: From lat- (to open) + ando (gate). An opening gate.

definitions: one who drives a ground vehicle
entries.etymology: From norta (to drive) + -no (agent). The one who drives.
From naur (fire) + -itar (master/operator). The fire-master.

definitions: the system that directs weapons to their target
entries.etymology: From naurë (fire/weapon) + sanmë (thought/calculation). Fire-thought; the thinking that guides the shot.

definitions: the process of identifying and tracking a target
entries.etymology: From centa (to point/direct) + tírië (watching) + -lmë (process). Directed watching.

definitions: the deliberate destruction of one's own vessel or equipment to deny it to the enemy
entries.etymology: From cirya (ship/device) + fira (to die) + -lë (act). The ship-death act; tech denial.

definitions: the act of launching combat craft from a carrier
entries.etymology: From cirya (ship) + lanta (to launch/fall forth). Ship-launch.

definitions: a concentrated offensive action against a target
entries.etymology: From rauca (to break) + hasta (to drive/project). A breaking drive; a concentrated attack.

definitions: the denial of movement or supply through a region
entries.etymology: From tië (path) + thal (shield/barrier) + -lë (action). Path-blocking.

definitions: the coordinated use of multiple military branches
entries.etymology: From ilya (all) + mahtië (fighting). All-fighting; every arm working as one.

definitions: a structural point designed to bear a weapon
entries.etymology: From naurion (cannon) + vestë (bond/mount). A weapon-bond point.

definitions: designed with parts that can be swapped
entries.etymology: From panta (to change/exchange) + -lë (quality). Changeable; made of exchangeable parts.

definitions: the work of keeping systems in order
entries.etymology: From cam- (to tend/care for) + nolë (craft/knowledge). The craft of tending.

definitions: the science of moving and sustaining forces
entries.etymology: From norta (to drive/transport) + cola (to bear). The bearing-and-driving; the chain of supply.

definitions: the delivery of fresh supplies to a force
entries.etymology: From en- (again) + anta (to give) + -ië (act). To give again; re-provisioning.

definitions: the positioning of forces for operations
entries.etymology: From hasta (to project) + norta (to drive) + -ië (act). Projected driving; putting forces where they must be.

definitions: the maximum distance a vessel or vehicle can operate
entries.etymology: From anda (long/far) + hastië (voyage) + -ië (measure). The measure of far-sailing.

definitions: the capacity to withstand damage and continue functioning
entries.etymology: From cuivëlë (energy/life) + tulcë (strength) + -ië (quality). Life-strength; the quality of enduring.

definitions: a ground vehicle that moves on linked track plates
entries.etymology: From norta (to drive) + cemë (ground/earth). A ground-driver; a crawling machine.

definitions: an electric motor that drives wheels or tracks
entries.etymology: From tulcalë (force) + norta (to drive). A force-driver.

definitions: the mechanism that absorbs terrain impact for a vehicle
entries.etymology: From lanc- (to hang/support) + -lë (system). The hanging system; what keeps the hull steady over rough ground.

definitions: relating to the energy of physical movement
entries.etymology: From tulcalë (force) + -ië (quality). Of force; pertaining to motion-energy.

definitions: the internal diameter of a weapon barrel
entries.etymology: From naurion (cannon) + andë (width/measure). The cannon's measure.

definitions: a region where control is actively disputed
entries.etymology: From mahta (to fight) + dorë (territory). Fought-over land.

definitions: a rotating assembly of blades that generates lift
entries.etymology: From querië (rotation) + ráma (wing/blade). A spinning wing.

definitions: a craft that flies by means of rotating blades
entries.etymology: From quërirámë (rotor) + cirya (craft). A rotor-craft.

definitions: the ability to rise straight upward without a runway
entries.etymology: From orta (to rise) + hastië (flight). Rising-flight; vertical departure.

definitions: the central structural body of an aircraft or vessel
entries.etymology: From cirya (craft) + hón (heart/core body). The craft's core body.

definitions: the forward-facing section of a craft
entries.etymology: From cirya (craft) + tië (path/direction). The path-facing part.

definitions: the rearward section of a craft
entries.etymology: From cirya (craft) + tellë (rear/end). The craft's end.

definitions: the enclosed station from which a craft is controlled
entries.etymology: From tir (to watch) + -ilmë (instrument) + mar (dwelling). The watcher's dwelling.

definitions: a pivoting mount that allows a weapon or sensor to track targets
entries.etymology: From querië (rotation) + centa (to direct). A rotating director.

definitions: the measured distance above a surface
entries.etymology: From halla (tall/high) + tírië (observation/measure). Height-measure.

definitions: remaining aloft in a fixed position
entries.etymology: From hara (to stay/remain) + tírië (watch). Staying in the air; watchful stillness aloft.

definitions: the length of time a craft can operate before returning
entries.etymology: From cuivëlë (energy/life) + andë (length). Energy-length; how long the power lasts.

definitions: to remain in an area awaiting orders or targets
entries.etymology: From hara (to stay) + hastië (flight). Staying-flight; circling and waiting.

definitions: a prearranged meeting of forces or vessels
entries.etymology: From omentië (meeting, gathering). An appointed meeting.

definitions: a weapon that accelerates subatomic particles to destructive effect
entries.etymology: From erthin (atom/particle) + tulcë (thrust/force). Atom-thrust; focused particle fire.

definitions: the explosive payload of a missile or torpedo
entries.etymology: From rauca (to break) + hón (heart/core). The breaking heart; the part that detonates.

definitions: an explosion that scatters lethal fragments
entries.etymology: From rauca (to break) + lanta (to scatter/fall). Breaking-scatter.

definitions: an explosion that kills through overpressure and heat
entries.etymology: From thúlë (breath/pressure) + raucë (violence). Breath-violence; killing by pressure wave.

definitions: the mechanism that steers a weapon to its target
entries.etymology: From tië (path) + hir (to find) + -ilmë (device). A path-finding device.

definitions: the sensor head of a guided weapon that locks onto a target
entries.etymology: From hir (to find) + -itar (master). The finding-master.

definitions: a communication link that passes targeting or sensor data between platforms
entries.etymology: From ista (knowledge) + hallë (bridge/span). A knowledge-bridge.

definitions: a burning countermeasure deployed to mislead heat-seeking weapons
entries.etymology: From uru (heat) + wanwa (illusion). A heat-illusion.

definitions: radar-reflective material deployed to confuse sensors
entries.etymology: From falma (wave) + wanwa (illusion). A wave-illusion.

definitions: a deployed cloud that blocks visual and sensor detection
entries.etymology: From nurta (to hide) + úsquë (smoke). Hiding-smoke.

definitions: a defensive system that detects and destroys incoming threats before impact
entries.etymology: From thal (shield) + tírië (watching) + nurta (to destroy/negate). A watching shield that destroys.

definitions: a system that determines whether a contact is allied or hostile
entries.etymology: From nossë (kin) + tir (to watch) + -ilmë (device). A kin-watching device.

definitions: the quality of being hidden from detection
entries.etymology: From nurta (to hide) + hastië (travel/state). Hidden-travel; moving unseen.

definitions: maneuvering to avoid weapons or detection
entries.etymology: From lerya (to free/escape) + hastië (flight). Escape-flight.

definitions: a deliberate change in position or course
entries.etymology: From tië (path) + panta (to change). Path-changing.
From lanc- (swift) + -ië (quality). Swiftness.

definitions: photonic energy given physical structure and rigidity
entries.etymology: From tulcalë (force) + cala (light). Force-light; light made solid.
From cala (light) + erthin (particle/atom). A light-particle.

definitions: a particle that carries fundamental forces
entries.etymology: From tulcalë (force) + erthin (particle). A force-particle.
From falma (wave) + notië (measurement). Wave-measure.

definitions: light below visible red, perceived as warmth
entries.etymology: From uru (heat) + cala (light). Heat-light.

definitions: light beyond the visible violet edge
entries.etymology: From aica (keen/sharp/piercing) + cala (light). Piercing-light.

definitions: the central thinking unit of a computer system
entries.etymology: From sanmë (thought) + hón (heart). The thought-heart.

definitions: a persistent signal emitted for navigation or identification
entries.etymology: From centa (signal) + calma (light/mark). A signal-light.

definitions: a transmission sent upward to an orbital or higher platform
entries.etymology: From ael (star/sky) + centië (signal-sending). Star-signaling.

definitions: an interconnected network of platforms sharing data
entries.etymology: From raicë (web/lattice) + istë (knowledge). A knowledge-web.
From parma (book) + -ëa (adjectival)

commonPhrases.meaning: A star shines upon the hour of our meeting (formal greeting)
May the sacred light guard you (blessing/dismissal)
What do you say? / What is your report?
Set course for the stars (naval command to depart)
Glory to the High Realm (patriotic/military salute)
The soul finds its still water (proverb about inner peace)
